ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_connections" (
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network connection */,
  "src" INTEGER /* Source node identifier */,
  "dst" INTEGER /* Destination node identifier */,
  "spec" INTEGER /* Specification identifier for the connection */,
  "slots_used" INTEGER /* Number of slots used in the connection */,
  "core_src" INTEGER /* Core identifier for the source node */,
  "core_dst" INTEGER /* Core identifier for the destination node */,
  "cores_used" INTEGER /* Number of cores used in the connection */,
  "tfk_slot" INTEGER /* Tuning fork slot identifier */,
  "connection_name" TEXT /* Human-readable name for the connection */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the connection (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the connection was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the connection was last updated */,
  "last_checked_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last health check */,
  "reliability_score" REAL /* A score indicating the reliability of the connection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_connections_core_dst FOREIGN KEY ("core_dst") REFERENCES core_resources ("core_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_connections_src FOREIGN KEY ("src") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_connections_core_src FOREIGN KEY ("core_src") REFERENCES core_resources ("core_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_connections_spec FOREIGN KEY ("spec") REFERENCES specifications ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_connections_dst FOREIGN KEY ("dst") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "node_type" TEXT /* Type of node (e.g., source, destination) */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the node */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location of the node */,
  "node_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the node */,
  "node_status" TEXT /* Current status of the node (e.g., online, offline, maintenance) */,
  "last_seen" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last activity or ping */,
  "uptime" REAL /* Total uptime of the node in hours */,
  "hardware_info" TEXT /* JSON column containing details about the hardware (e.g., model, manufacturer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "specifications" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specification */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the specification */,
  "bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth capacity in Mbps */,
  "latency" REAL /* Latency in milliseconds */,
  "spec_name" TEXT /* Human-readable name for the specification */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the specification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the specification was last updated */,
  "max_bandwidth" REAL /* Maximum bandwidth capacity in Mbps */,
  "min_bandwidth" REAL /* Minimum bandwidth capacity in Mbps */,
  "max_latency" REAL /* Maximum latency in milliseconds */,
  "min_latency" REAL /* Minimum latency in milliseconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "core_resources" (
  "core_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each core resource */,
  "core_type" TEXT /* Type of core (e.g., CPU, GPU) */,
  "core_capacity" REAL /* Capacity of the core in processing units */,
  "core_status" TEXT /* Current status of the core (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "core_name" TEXT /* Human-readable name for the core resource */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the core resource was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the core resource was last updated */,
  "last_checked_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last health check */,
  "uptime" REAL /* Total uptime of the core resource in hours */,
  "hardware_info" TEXT /* JSON column containing details about the hardware (e.g., model, manufacturer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("core_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "slot_usage" (
  "slot_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each slot usage record */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated connection */,
  "usage" INTEGER /* Current usage of the slot */,
  "max_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum capacity of the slot */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the slot (e.g., active, inactive, reserved) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the slot usage record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the slot usage record was last updated */,
  "last_checked_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last health check */,
  "reliability_score" REAL /* A score indicating the reliability of the slot */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("slot_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_slot_usage_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES network_connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for user login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network engineer, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the resource */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the connection accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access log was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES network_connections ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
